userStories.title: Initialize Vite, React, and TypeScript workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean Vite, React, and TypeScript project so that the application can be developed locally and deployed as a static website.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dev starts a local development server
npm run build creates a production build
npm run preview serves the built output
The default page loads in a browser
The project builds without TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Add linting, formatting, typecheck, and unit test scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want baseline engineering quality checks so that every later ticket has a clear definition of done.
acceptanceCriteria: ESLint is configured for React and TypeScript
At least one sample unit test passes

userStories.title: Create repository folder structure and code boundaries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clear project structure so that future changes remain maintainable and easy for junior developers to navigate.

userStories.title: Create responsive application shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean responsive page shell so that the site works on mobile, tablet, and desktop devices.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scrolling on mobile widths
userStories.notes: Feature pages plug into this shell.

userStories.title: Define typed card domain model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a strict internal card model so that card content is consistent across all features.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier type exists with Open, Working, Deep

userStories.title: Load and parse card_deck.csv
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to load the deck from CSV so that non-developers can maintain card content.
acceptanceCriteria: CSV file is imported at build time
Rows are parsed into typed card objects
Invalid rows fail with readable errors

userStories.title: Normalize and validate card dataset
userStories.description: As a developer, I want imported card data normalized so that the application uses safe and predictable content.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown category values are rejected or mapped intentionally
Cards are sorted by card number

userStories.title: Create reusable visual card component
userStories.description: As a user, I want cards to look like premium physical deck cards so that the site feels engaging and intentional.
acceptanceCriteria: Card shows flavour text when present

userStories.title: Add tier visual system
userStories.description: As a user, I want Open, Working, and Deep cards clearly differentiated so that I understand conversation depth before selecting a card.

userStories.title: Create browse all cards page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse the complete deck so that I can intentionally choose discussion prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Cards display in responsive grid or list
Loading empty state exists if data missing

userStories.title: Add category filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter by card category so that I can focus on relevant conversation themes.

userStories.title: Add tier filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter by Open, Working, or Deep so that I can control discussion intensity.

userStories.title: Add text search
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search the deck so that I can quickly find relevant prompts.

userStories.title: Create single random draw mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw a random card so that the deck can surprise me with useful conversation prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: One valid random card is shown
Previously drawn card is not repeated until full deck cycle completes or reset is used

userStories.title: Create Draw Three Keep One mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want the recommended 1:1 ritual available digitally so that I can run meetings directly from the site.

userStories.title: Add pairing references
userStories.description: As a user, I want to discover paired follow-up cards so that longer conversations can continue naturally.
acceptanceCriteria: PairsWith references are read from data
No pairing section shown when empty

userStories.title: Create Play Guide page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand how to use the deck so that I can run better conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Sections for structure, ways to play, rituals, etiquette exist
Readable typography on mobile and desktop

userStories.title: Persist lightweight preferences locally
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want the site to remember my last used settings so that I do not reconfigure every visit.

userStories.title: Add accessibility and keyboard navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the site accessible by keyboard and assistive technologies so that more people can use it.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive controls are keyboard reachable

userStories.title: Deploy static site to Vercel
userStories.description: As a stakeholder, I want the website publicly available so that the deck can be shared and used immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Client-side routes resolve correctly after refresh
No console errors on landing page
Random draw mode works in production
